name: openai-whisper
tagline: ClawHub (334★): Local speech-to-text with the Whisper CLI (no API key).
verdict: Strong core: quality A, security A. The main gaps are housekeeping -- no version or changelog to track changes, and the Homebrew install isn't pinned to a version (though it's the standard registry with an exact, well-known formula name, so typosquat risk is low).

highlights.summary: No version field in frontmatter, no changelog section, and no dated revision notes anywhere in the file.
Install source is the standard Homebrew registry with an exact, well-known formula name (no typosquat, no ad-hoc registry) but no version is pinned — matches the rubric's 'well-kno
States capability and technology (speech-to-text, Whisper CLI, no API key) but has no explicit 'use when' cue or example user phrasing — says what it does, not when to invoke it.
